skill.name: epistemic-checkpoint
skill.description: Requires fact verification before answering questions about current versions, release status, dates, packages, or API changes.
skill.summary: Verify time-sensitive technical facts before using them in an answer.
skill.author: ANcpLua

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution and blocker alerts were false positives. The file contains fenced text examples and instructions to use local assertions and WebSearch, but no executable scripts, shell calls, weak cryptography, or prompt injection were found.

low_findings.title: Fenced Text Misidentified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The static Ruby or shell backtick detections are false positives. The cited areas are Markdown fenced text blocks and example response text, not executable code or scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are inside SKILL.md Markdown examples. No Ruby code, shell backticks, subprocess calls, or executable files are present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alert Is Not Supported
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptographic algorithm alert at the metadata description is a false positive. The cited line contains descriptive YAML metadata and no hash, cipher, or cryptographic API usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 begins the skill description block. There is no cryptographic term or implementation at that location.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alert Is Not Supported
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance alert is a false positive. The cited line advises verification when user-provided release status may be wrong, and it does not inspect host, process, network, or account details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a table entry about factual verification. No system inventory, environment probing, or reconnaissance command is present.

low_findings.title: Uses Local File Read and Web Search
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to read a local assertions file and use WebSearch for current facts. This is expected behavior for the skill, but users should understand that it may access local project knowledge and external sources.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly name a local assertions file and WebSearch. The behavior is limited to verification and shows no exfiltration intent.

content.value_statement: Technical answers often fail when model memory is stale about versions, releases, and dates. This skill adds a verification checkpoint using local assertions and official web sources before the assistant answers.
actual_capabilities: Triggers when a request depends on current versions, dates, package releases, or API status.
Directs the assistant to check a local assertions file before relying on model memory.
Directs the assistant to use WebSearch when local assertions do not answer the question.
Prioritizes official documentation sources for software release verification.
Structures answers with the claim checked, source used, verified fact, and confidence level.
Encourages exact version and release status language in technical answers.
limitations: Requires access to the referenced local assertions file for the first verification step.
Depends on WebSearch availability when local assertions do not cover the topic.
Does not validate source quality beyond preferring official documentation.
Does not perform automated package registry queries or execute verification scripts.

use_cases.title: Check Framework Release Status
use_cases.description: Verify whether a framework version is stable, preview, deprecated, or current before recommending implementation patterns.

use_cases.title: Prepare Accurate Upgrade Guidance
use_cases.description: Confirm package and runtime versions before writing migration advice or upgrade notes.

use_cases.title: Reduce Stale Documentation Answers
use_cases.description: Force a source-backed checkpoint before answering documentation questions that may have changed after model training.

prompt_templates.title: Verify a Version
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner check for one current software version.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use epistemic-checkpoint to verify the current stable version of [software]. Include the source checked, verified fact, and confidence level.

prompt_templates.title: Check Release Status
prompt_templates.scenario: Confirm whether a release is preview, stable, LTS, deprecated, or unsupported.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use epistemic-checkpoint before answering. Verify whether [software version] is [claimed status], then explain the correct release status.

prompt_templates.title: Validate Upgrade Advice
prompt_templates.scenario: Review guidance that depends on changing package or platform versions.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use epistemic-checkpoint to verify the version assumptions behind this upgrade plan: [paste plan]. Then identify any stale assumptions.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Time-Sensitive Claims
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of several technical claims before publication.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use epistemic-checkpoint to audit these time-sensitive claims: [claims]. Check local assertions first, then official sources, and report confidence for each claim.

output_examples.output: The answer starts by naming the claim, then cites an official source check. It states the verified React 19 status and continues with guidance based on that confirmed status.

output_examples.input: Can I target the latest .NET release for a new service?
output_examples.output: The answer verifies the current .NET release and support status first. It then recommends a target version using the verified baseline.

output_examples.input: Review this migration note for stale version assumptions.
output_examples.output: The response lists each assumption, the source checked, the verified fact, and a confidence rating. It flags any claim that needs revision.

best_practices: Use official vendor documentation when web verification is required.
Record the date of verification for release status and support lifecycle facts.
Separate verified facts from recommendations that depend on those facts.
anti_patterns: Answering current-version questions from model memory alone.
Treating user-provided release status as true without verification.
Using vague words such as latest or current without naming an exact version.

faq.question: What does this skill verify?
faq.answer: It verifies time-sensitive technical facts such as versions, release status, dates, package versions, and API deprecations.

faq.question: Does it execute commands?
faq.answer: No executable code is included. The skill contains Markdown instructions for local file reading and web search.

faq.question: Why does it check a local assertions file?
faq.answer: The local assertions file can store trusted project facts that should be checked before using web sources.

faq.question: When should I use WebSearch with this skill?
faq.answer: Use WebSearch when the local assertions file does not contain the fact needed for a time-sensitive answer.

faq.question: Can this replace expert review?
faq.answer: No. It improves source checking, but expert review is still needed for important technical decisions.

faq.question: Which assistants can use it?
faq.answer: The marketplace metadata lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
